title: Run the Service as a Web Service
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries
Deploying Server Pages and Web Services on iSeries
Run the Service as a Web Service
text: Run the Service as a Web Service After you have confirmed that the test service websrv.svc runs correctly, you can deploy it as a web service. To do so, you need to generate a WSDL file that describes the interface to web service clients that wish to invoke the service, and then configure the WRD to use this file. Create the .wsdl by running ide.exe on Windows with the following command line: ide.exe /sto /mwr=ws WEBSRV This creates a file named websrv.wsdl in the current directory. Configure the WRD to use this file. You must move the file to the WEB-INF/wsdl IFS directory and (re)build and (re)deploy the wrd.war file. For more information, see Configure the WRD and Create a wrd.war File on iSeries Check that the Uniface Soap Request Dispatcher (SRD, included in the WRD jar file) can access it by starting a web browser and entering: http:// iSeriesNetworkName :10000/uniface/services/websrv.wsdl Note: We assume that your port number is 10000 and your context root is /uniface . You should see the contents of the wsdl file in your browser. This proves that the SRD is able to access the file. If you get an error message, the SRD is not able to access it, so check that the wsdl file is in the correct place, and that it is included in the wrd.war file. At this point, the service is a web service and can be invoked by web services clients. If you want to test it with Uniface Web services call-out on Windows, you can import the Web service wsdl by running ide.exe : This command reads the wsdl provided by the SRD on your iSeries and creates a SOAP signature called WEBSRVSERVICE. ide.exe /sti /mwr=ws http://iSeriesNetworkName:10000/uniface/services/websrv.wsdl Copy Use the same form component to test it. Alter the ProcScript in the command button’s detail trigger: trigger detail activate "WEBSRVSERVICE".callin(TF1, TF2) D1=$status end; detail Copy In the [DRIVER_SETTINGS] section of the IDE assignment file ide.asn , add SOP = U2.0 Start the IDE again, and recompile and test your form in the same way as before. This time, when you click the button, IDE acts as a web services client and the following occurs: It retrieves the wsdl for WEBSRV from the SRD Uses it to construct a SOAP message Sends it to the iSeries HTTP server The IBM i integrated application Server runs the SRD The SRD forwards the request to the UROUTER The UROUTER starts a USERVER process The USERVER instance carries out the process Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
